---
title: "Day 2 — Methodological Exploration"
subtitle: "Tuesday 16 June 2026"
---

## Theme

*Creating practical pathways for cross-disciplinary learning through active collaboration*

Day 2 is the first full day of hands-on analysis. Groups explore their case study data, develop analysis plans, begin implementation, and share preliminary findings with the partner group working on the same case study.

---

## Schedule

| Time | Activity |
|------|----------|
| 9:00–10:00 | Small group work: explore data, develop analysis plans, begin implementation |
| 10:00–10:30 | Morning break |
| 10:30–12:00 | Continue analyses · Prepare preliminary findings |
| 12:00–12:30 | **Keynote:** IDM perspective — case studies illustrating challenges and areas for collaboration |
| 12:30–14:00 | Lunch |
| 14:00–15:30 | **Intergroup session:** Groups working on the same case study present and compare analysis plans · Groups separate and refine |
| 15:30–16:00 | Afternoon break |
| 16:00–17:00 | **Plenary:** Discuss both case studies with everyone |
| 18:30 | Conference dinner |

---

## Learning Objectives

By the end of Day 2, participants will be able to:

1. Apply at least one SA technique (competing risks, multi-state models, or frailty models) to an infectious disease problem
2. Apply at least one IDM approach (compartmental modeling or data synthesis) to the case study data
3. Identify practical challenges encountered when applying methods cross-disciplinarily
4. Give and receive constructive feedback on analysis plans from a complementary disciplinary perspective

---

## Methods in Focus

### Survival Analysis Methods

- **Competing risks:** handling multiple event types (infection, hospitalization, death)
- **Multi-state models:** modeling transitions through disease states (S → E → I → R/D)
- **Frailty models:** capturing unobserved heterogeneity in susceptibility or infectiousness

### Infectious Disease Modeling Methods

- **Compartmental SEIRD models:** mechanistic representation of epidemic dynamics
- **Data synthesis:** combining aggregate surveillance data with mechanistic assumptions
- **Reproduction number estimation:** Rt estimation from case count time series

---

## Materials

### Keynote Slides (IDM Perspective)

*To be posted after the workshop*

### Group Analysis Plans

*Groups will share their analysis plan documents here*

### Code Examples

*Preliminary code from group sessions will be shared here*
